---
title: "Electricity Export with HVDC"
---

## Role in the Value Chain

The electricity architecture is the reference case. Wind turbine output is conditioned for AC infield collection, aggregated offshore, converted to HVDC for export, and converted back to AC at shore.

## Component Chain

1. turbine generator
2. turbine rectifier, inverter, and transformer
3. 66 kV AC infield cable network
4. offshore AC substation and HVDC converter platform
5. HVDC export cable
6. onshore converter station and grid connection

## Modelling Implications

The key modelling feature is that export infrastructure is sized for peak installed capacity. HVDC platforms and export cables therefore contribute strongly to the capacity-linked cost term.

The current assumption set uses a 2 GW HVDC export basis. This creates discrete export capacity increments and makes under-utilisation of peak-sized infrastructure important in the LCOE calculation.

## Related Components

- [Generator and Power Electronics](../turbine_system/generator_and_power_electronics.qmd)
- [Infield AC Cables](../electrical_infra/infield_ac_cables.qmd)
- [Offshore AC Substation](../electrical_infra/offshore_ac_substation.qmd)
- [HVDC Converter Stations](../electrical_infra/hvdc_converter_stations.qmd)
- [HVDC Export Cables](../electrical_infra/hvdc_export_cables.qmd)
